title: Art Market in Decline
passage:
The longest bull run in a century of art-market history ended on a dramatic note with a sale of 56 works by Damien Hirst, ‘Beautiful Inside My Head Forever’, at Sotheby’s in London on September 15th 2008. All but two pieces sold, fetching more than £70m, a record for a sale by a single artist. It was a last hurrah. As the auctioneer called out bids, in New York one of the oldest banks on Wall Street, Lehman Brothers, filed for bankruptcy.

The world art market had already been losing momentum for a while after rising vertiginously since 2003. At its peak in 2007 it was worth some $65 billion, double the figure five years earlier. Since then it may have come down to $50 billion. But the market generates interest far beyond its size because it brings together great wealth, enormous egos, greed, passion and controversy in a way matched by few other industries.

In the weeks and months that followed Mr Hirst’s sale, spending of any sort became deeply unfashionable, especially in New York, where the bail-out of the banks coincided with the loss of thousands of jobs and the financial demise of many art-buying investors. In the art world that meant collectors stayed away from galleries and salerooms. Sales of contemporary art fell by two-thirds, and in the most overheated sector—for Chinese contemporary art—they were down by nearly 90% in the year to November 2008.

The current downturn in the art market is the worst since the Japanese stopped buying Impressionists at the end of 1989. This time experts reckon that prices are about 40% down on their peak on average. But Edward Dolman, Christie’s chief executive, says: ‘I’m pretty confident we’re at the bottom.’ What makes this slump different from the last, he says, is that there are still buyers in the market. Almost everyone interviewed for this report said that the biggest problem at the moment is not a lack of demand but a lack of good work to sell. The three Ds—death, debt and divorce—still deliver works of art to the market. But anyone who does not have to sell is keeping away, waiting for confidence to return.

questions.questionText: In the first paragraph, Damien Hirst's sale was referred to as ‘a last hurrah’ because ____.

options.A: the art market had witnessed a succession of victories
options.B: the auctioneer finally got the two pieces at the highest bids
options.C: Beautiful Inside My Head Forever won over all masterpieces
options.D: it was successfully made just before the world financial crisis

questions.explanation: 拍卖发生的同时雷曼兄弟申请破产，说明这是金融危机来临前艺术市场的最后一次辉煌。
questions.commonMistake: 忽略时间线，把 last hurrah 误解为作品本身的胜利。

questions.questionText: By saying ‘spending of any sort became deeply unfashionable’, the author suggests that ____.

options.A: collectors were no longer actively involved in art-market auctions
options.B: people stopped every kind of spending and stayed away from galleries
options.C: art collection as a fashion had lost its appeal to a great extent
options.D: works of art in general had gone out of fashion so they were not worth buying

questions.explanation: 后文直接说明收藏者不再去画廊和拍卖场，A 是对该句在艺术市场语境中的准确转述。
questions.commonMistake: 把 any sort 绝对化，误选‘所有人停止一切消费’。

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is NOT true?

options.A: Sales of contemporary art fell dramatically from 2007 to 2008.
options.B: The art market surpassed many other industries in momentum.
options.C: The market generally went downward in various ways.
options.D: Some art dealers were awaiting better chances to come.

questions.explanation: 原文说艺术市场因财富和争议而受到超出其规模的关注，并未说它在发展势头上超过其他产业。
questions.commonMistake: 把 ‘interest far beyond its size’ 偷换成 ‘momentum surpassed industries’。

questions.questionText: The three Ds mentioned in the last paragraph are ____.
questions.explanation: death、debt 和 divorce 会迫使作品进入市场出售，因此是促进艺术品流通的因素。
questions.commonMistake: 只记住缩写，没有结合 deliver works of art to the market 判断含义。

questions.questionText: The most appropriate title for this text could be ____.
questions.explanation: 全文围绕金融危机后艺术市场下行、成交萎缩及其原因展开，C 概括最完整。
questions.commonMistake: 被某一段的拍卖细节带偏，忽略全文的下行主线。
